message:
fix(util/det_hash): enforce little-endian encoding and width-independent hashing

DetHasher used to_ne_bytes() for write_u16/u32/u64/u128, making hash
values depend on host byte order. Two nodes in a distributed system
running the same code on different architectures (LE vs BE) would
compute different hashes for the same logical value, breaking consensus
and deduplication protocols that rely on deterministic hashing.

Additionally, the default Hasher::write_usize() implementation hashes
usize as its native width (4 bytes on 32-bit, 8 bytes on 64-bit),
meaning the same Rust value hashed differently across architectures.

Changes:
- All to_ne_bytes() calls replaced with to_le_bytes()
- Explicit write_usize() that always casts to u64 before hashing,
  ensuring 32-bit and 64-bit nodes produce identical hashes
- Explicit signed integer methods (write_i8 through write_isize) that
  delegate to their unsigned counterparts via as-cast, ensuring signed
  and unsigned representations of the same bit pattern hash identically

New integration test (tests/det_hash_portability.rs) verifies:
- write_u32(x) produces the same hash as write(&x.to_le_bytes())
- write_usize(x) produces the same hash as write_u64(x as u64)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
